rationale: Unit and integration evidence must stay mostly green to trust backend coverage outputs.
Blocking failures in high-risk flows should not be masked by unrelated infrastructure constraints.
Midterm coverage should materially improve visibility across the five highest-risk modules.
Empirical analysis becomes unreliable when too many tests oscillate across runs.
Smoke feedback should remain useful even when optional live services are not enabled.

actual: The thresholds are balanced: strict enough to catch regressions without blocking the repo on known runtime-dependent API gaps.
The remaining observed failures are dominated by infrastructure/runtime availability rather than functional regressions in mock-backed flows.
Yes. At least one high-risk module still has less than 10% backend coverage, which means the test portfolio is still thin in those areas.
